description: SpyCloud Sentinel Analytics Rules - 49 scheduled detection rules across 8 categories. Merge into mainTemplate resources array.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Infostealer Credential Exposure Detected
properties.description: Detects new infostealer-sourced credential exposures (severity >= 20) from SpyCloud. These indicate a device was actively infected with malware that exfiltrated credentials, cookies, and session tokens. Severity 25 includes application-level data enabling MFA bypass. Requires immediate device investigation and credential reset.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(infected_machine_id)
| summarize
    ExposureCount = count(),
    Emails = make_set(email, 10),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10),
    MaxSeverity = max(severity),
    LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated)
  by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, source_id
| extend HostCustomEntity = user_hostname, AccountCustomEntity = tostring(Emails[0])

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Plaintext Password Exposure (Immediate Credential Reset Required)
properties.description: Alerts when SpyCloud finds credentials with plaintext (unencrypted) passwords. These credentials can be used immediately by attackers without cracking. Highest priority for password reset.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(password_plaintext)
| summarize
    ExposureCount = count(),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10),
    LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated)
  by email, email_domain
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Stolen Session Cookies Detected (MFA Bypass Risk)
properties.description: Detects when stolen browser cookies or session tokens are found in SpyCloud data (severity 25). Attackers can replay authenticated sessions to bypass MFA entirely. Requires immediate session revocation and device investigation.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity == 25
| summarize
    ExposureCount = count(),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 15),
    Devices = make_set(infected_machine_id, 5)
  by email, user_hostname
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, HostCustomEntity = user_hostname

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Sensitive PII Exposure (SSN, Financial, or Health Data)
properties.description: Detects exposure records containing sensitive PII such as Social Security numbers, bank account data, tax IDs, or health insurance IDs. May trigger breach notification obligations under HIPAA, PCI DSS, GDPR, or state privacy laws. Requires immediate compliance team notification.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 5
| where breach_category has_any ("pii", "financial", "health")
    or breach_title has_any ("ssn", "national_id", "tax", "bank", "financial", "health")
| summarize
    ExposureCount = count(),
    BreachSources = make_set(breach_title, 10),
    Categories = make_set(breach_category, 5)
  by email, email_domain
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Executive or VIP User Credential Exposure
properties.description: Detects credential exposures for users listed in the SpyCloud-VIP-Watchlist. These high-value accounts (executives, board members, IT admins) are prime targets for spear phishing and business email compromise. Escalate immediately to executive protection team.
properties.query:
let vip_users = _GetWatchlist('SpyCloud-VIP-Watchlist') | project SearchKey;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where email in (vip_users) or email_username in (vip_users)
| summarize
    ExposureCount = count(),
    MaxSeverity = max(severity),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10),
    BreachSources = make_set(breach_title, 5)
  by email, email_domain
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Multi-Domain Credential Reuse Detected (5+ Services)
properties.description: Detects when a single user has credentials exposed across 5 or more different target domains, indicating widespread credential reuse or a heavily infected device. High risk of account takeover across multiple services simultaneously.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| summarize
    TargetDomainCount = dcount(target_domain),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 20),
    MaxSeverity = max(severity),
    HasPlaintext = countif(isnotempty(password_plaintext))
  by email
| where TargetDomainCount >= 5
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Device Re-Infection After Previous Remediation
properties.description: Detects when the same infected_machine_id appears in new SpyCloud exposures after previous remediation was logged in the MDE table. Indicates the device was re-compromised after initial cleanup, suggesting persistent malware, rootkit, or incomplete remediation.
properties.query:
let remediated_devices = Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL
| where ActionStatus == "Success"
| distinct InfectedMachineId;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where infected_machine_id in (remediated_devices)
| summarize
    NewExposures = count(),
    LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10)
  by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, email
| extend HostCustomEntity = user_hostname, AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- High-Sighting Credential (Actively Traded on Dark Web)
properties.description: Detects credentials with a sighting count greater than 3, meaning the same email/password combination has been observed across multiple independent breach sources. Dramatically increases probability of active credential stuffing attacks using this pair.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where sighting > 3
| summarize
    MaxSighting = max(sighting),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10),
    PasswordTypes = make_set(password_type, 5),
    LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated)
  by email
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Password Reuse Across Critical Systems
properties.description: Detects when the same password hash appears across multiple target domains for the same user, proving credential reuse. When combined with plaintext exposure on any one site, all sites sharing that password are immediately compromisable.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(password)
| summarize
    DomainCount = dcount(target_domain),
    Domains = make_set(target_domain, 20),
    HasPlaintext = max(iff(isnotempty(password_plaintext), 1, 0))
  by email, password
| where DomainCount >= 3
| project email, DomainCount, Domains, HasPlaintext
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- New Malware Family Identified in Breach Catalog
properties.description: Detects when a previously unseen malware family appears in the SpyCloud breach catalog. New malware families may indicate zero-day threats, new criminal toolkits, or emerging threat actor campaigns that require proactive hunting in your environment.
properties.query:
let known_families = SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
| where TimeGenerated < ago(1d)
| where isnotempty(malware_family)
| distinct malware_family;
SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where isnotempty(malware_family)
| where malware_family !in (known_families)
| project TimeGenerated, malware_family, short_title, site, num_records, confidence, breach_category
| extend DomainCustomEntity = site

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Sensitive Source Breach Detection
properties.description: Detects breaches flagged as sensitive sources in the SpyCloud catalog. Sensitive sources often involve government, military, healthcare, or critical infrastructure data with restricted distribution under TLP protocols.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
| where sensitive_source == true
| project TimeGenerated, short_title, site, breach_category, num_records, confidence, tlp, spycloud_publish_date
| extend DomainCustomEntity = site

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Credential Exposure Volume Spike (Anomaly)
properties.description: Detects when the daily credential exposure count exceeds 3 standard deviations above the 14-day rolling average, indicating a potential mass breach event, new watchlist domain being targeted, or bulk credential dump affecting your organization.
properties.query:
let baseline = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated between (ago(14d) .. ago(1d))
| summarize DailyCount = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| summarize AvgDaily = avg(DailyCount), StdDev = stdev(DailyCount);
let threshold = toscalar(baseline | project AvgDaily + 3 * StdDev);
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize TodayCount = count()
| where TodayCount > threshold
| extend AlertDetail = strcat("Today: ", TodayCount, " exposures vs threshold: ", threshold)

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- First-Time Domain Exposure Detected
properties.description: Detects when credentials from a corporate email domain appear in SpyCloud for the first time. This is a leading indicator that a new breach has affected your organization or a previously unmonitored domain has been compromised.
properties.query:
let known_domains = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated < ago(1d)
| distinct email_domain;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where email_domain !in (known_domains)
| where isnotempty(email_domain)
| summarize
    ExposureCount = count(),
    UniqueEmails = dcount(email),
    Emails = make_set(email, 10),
    MaxSeverity = max(severity)
  by email_domain
| extend DomainCustomEntity = email_domain

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Stale Credential Without Remediation (30+ Days)
properties.description: Detects credential exposures older than 30 days that have no corresponding remediation entry in the Conditional Access logs, indicating the compromised credential may still be active and usable by attackers.
properties.query:
let remediated = SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL
| distinct Email;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where TimeGenerated < ago(30d)
| where email !in (remediated)
| summarize
    ExposureAge = datetime_diff('day', now(), min(TimeGenerated)),
    ExposureCount = count(),
    MaxSeverity = max(severity),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10)
  by email, infected_machine_id
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Corporate Email Found in Consumer Site Breach
properties.description: Detects when corporate email addresses appear in breaches of consumer services (social media, gaming, dating, shopping). Indicates employees reusing corporate credentials on personal sites, creating lateral attack vectors into the enterprise.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| join kind=inner (
    SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
    | where consumer_category has_any ("social", "gaming", "dating", "shopping", "entertainment", "adult", "streaming")
    | project source_id = toint(replace_string(uuid, "-", "")), consumer_category, short_title
) on source_id
| summarize
    ConsumerSites = make_set(short_title, 10),
    Categories = make_set(consumer_category, 5),
    ExposureCount = count()
  by email, email_domain
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Compromised Credential Used in Successful Sign-In
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud credential exposures with successful Azure AD sign-ins within 24 hours. A successful sign-in after credential exposure strongly suggests an attacker has tested or used the stolen credentials. Immediate session revocation and password reset required.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| project ExposureTime = TimeGenerated, email, severity, source_id, infected_machine_id
| join kind=inner (
    SigninLogs
    | where ResultType == 0
    | project SignInTime = TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Location = LocationDetails.city, AppDisplayName, DeviceDetail
) on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| where SignInTime between (ExposureTime .. (ExposureTime + 24h))
| project ExposureTime, SignInTime, email, severity, IPAddress, Location, AppDisplayName, infected_machine_id
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = IPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User with Entra ID Risky Sign-In
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposures with Azure AD Identity Protection risky sign-in events. When a user with known compromised credentials also triggers a risk detection (anomalous IP, leaked credentials, malware-linked IP), this represents high-confidence account compromise.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    AADUserRiskEvents
    | where RiskLevel in ("high", "medium")
    | project UserPrincipalName, RiskLevel, RiskState, RiskDetail, DetectedDateTime = TimeGenerated, IPAddress, Activity
) on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| project email, RiskLevel, RiskState, RiskDetail, DetectedDateTime, IPAddress, Activity
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = IPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User Impossible Travel Sign-In Detected
properties.description: Detects impossible travel patterns for users with SpyCloud credential exposures. When a user's credentials are exposed AND sign-ins originate from geographically impossible locations within short timeframes, this strongly indicates credential theft and active exploitation by a remote attacker.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    SigninLogs
    | where ResultType == 0
    | project UserPrincipalName, TimeGenerated, IPAddress, Lat = toreal(LocationDetails.geoCoordinates.latitude), Lon = toreal(LocationDetails.geoCoordinates.longitude), City = tostring(LocationDetails.city), Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
) on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| sort by email asc, TimeGenerated asc
| serialize
| extend PrevTime = prev(TimeGenerated, 1), PrevLat = prev(Lat, 1), PrevLon = prev(Lon, 1), PrevUser = prev(email, 1)
| where email == PrevUser
| extend TimeDiffHours = datetime_diff('hour', TimeGenerated, PrevTime)
| extend DistanceKm = geo_distance_2points(Lon, Lat, PrevLon, PrevLat) / 1000
| where TimeDiffHours > 0 and TimeDiffHours < 4
| where DistanceKm > 1000
| project email, TimeGenerated, IPAddress, City, Country, TimeDiffHours, DistanceKm
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = IPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User MFA Registration Change (Account Takeover Risk)
properties.description: Detects when a user with known SpyCloud credential exposure registers a new MFA method or modifies existing MFA settings. Attackers who gain access via stolen credentials often register their own MFA device to maintain persistent access. Requires immediate verification with the user.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    AuditLogs
    | where OperationName has_any ("User registered security info", "User changed default security info", "Admin registered security info", "User registered all required security info")
    | extend TargetUPN = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
    | project TargetUPN, OperationName, MFATime = TimeGenerated, InitiatedBy = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
) on $left.email == $right.TargetUPN
| project email, OperationName, MFATime, InitiatedBy
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User Suspicious Mailbox Rule Created
properties.description: Detects when a user with known SpyCloud credential exposure creates mailbox forwarding or inbox rules in Office 365. Attackers frequently set up mail forwarding to exfiltrate emails or hide evidence of compromise by deleting security notifications.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    OfficeActivity
    | where Operation in ("New-InboxRule", "Set-InboxRule", "Set-Mailbox", "New-TransportRule")
    | where Parameters has_any ("ForwardTo", "ForwardAsAttachmentTo", "RedirectTo", "DeleteMessage", "MoveToFolder")
    | project UserId, Operation, Parameters = tostring(Parameters), RuleTime = TimeGenerated, ClientIP
) on $left.email == $right.UserId
| project email, Operation, Parameters, RuleTime, ClientIP
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = ClientIP

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User Granted OAuth Application Consent
properties.description: Detects when a user with SpyCloud credential exposure consents to a new OAuth application. Attackers with stolen credentials often consent to malicious OAuth apps to establish persistent access that survives password resets, enabling long-term data exfiltration.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    AuditLogs
    | where OperationName == "Consent to application"
    | extend TargetUPN = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
    | extend AppName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
    | project TargetUPN, AppName, ConsentTime = TimeGenerated, InitiatedBy = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
) on $left.email == $right.TargetUPN
| project email, AppName, ConsentTime, InitiatedBy
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User Granted Administrative Role (Privilege Escalation)
properties.description: Detects when a user with SpyCloud credential exposure is assigned an administrative role in Azure AD. This could indicate privilege escalation by an attacker who gained access through stolen credentials, or an insider threat where compromised credentials are used to elevate permissions.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    AuditLogs
    | where OperationName has_any ("Add member to role", "Add eligible member to role")
    | extend TargetUPN = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
    | extend RoleName = tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties[1].newValue)
    | project TargetUPN, RoleName, RoleTime = TimeGenerated, InitiatedBy = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
) on $left.email == $right.TargetUPN
| project email, RoleName, RoleTime, InitiatedBy
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User Self-Service Password Change
properties.description: Detects when a user with known SpyCloud credential exposure performs a self-service password change. While this could be legitimate remediation, it could also indicate an attacker using stolen credentials to change the password and lock out the legitimate user.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    AuditLogs
    | where OperationName in ("Change user password", "Reset user password", "Change password (self-service)")
    | extend TargetUPN = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
    | project TargetUPN, OperationName, ChangeTime = TimeGenerated, InitiatedBy = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName), Result = Result
) on $left.email == $right.TargetUPN
| project email, OperationName, ChangeTime, InitiatedBy, Result
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User Initiated eDiscovery or Content Search Export
properties.description: Detects when a user with SpyCloud credential exposure initiates eDiscovery searches or content exports in Microsoft 365. This is a high-severity data exfiltration indicator since eDiscovery provides access to all organizational email and documents.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    OfficeActivity
    | where Operation in ("SearchStarted", "SearchExportDownloaded", "ViewedSearchExported", "New-ComplianceSearch", "Start-ComplianceSearch")
    | project UserId, Operation, SearchTime = TimeGenerated, ClientIP
) on $left.email == $right.UserId
| project email, Operation, SearchTime, ClientIP
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = ClientIP

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User SharePoint Mass Download (Data Exfiltration Risk)
properties.description: Detects when a user with SpyCloud credential exposure downloads a large number of SharePoint/OneDrive files within a short window. This pattern indicates potential data exfiltration by an attacker using stolen credentials to bulk-harvest corporate documents.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    OfficeActivity
    | where Operation in ("FileDownloaded", "FileSyncDownloadedFull")
    | where OfficeWorkload in ("SharePoint", "OneDrive")
    | summarize DownloadCount = count(), Files = make_set(SourceFileName, 20) by UserId, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h), ClientIP
    | where DownloadCount >= 50
) on $left.email == $right.UserId
| project email, DownloadCount, Files, TimeGenerated, ClientIP
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = ClientIP

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- UEBA Anomalous Behavior from Exposed User
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud credential exposures with UEBA anomalous behavior detections. When a user with stolen credentials also triggers behavioral anomalies (unusual resource access, atypical sign-in patterns, anomalous data access), this represents high-confidence account compromise.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    BehaviorAnalytics
    | where ActivityInsights has_any ("True", "Anomalous")
    | project UserPrincipalName, ActivityType, ActivityInsights, InvestigationPriority, SourceIPAddress, ActionType, TimeGenerated
    | where InvestigationPriority > 3
) on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| project email, ActivityType, ActivityInsights, InvestigationPriority, SourceIPAddress, ActionType, TimeGenerated
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SourceIPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User First-Time Access to Sensitive Resource
properties.description: Detects when a user with SpyCloud credential exposure accesses a resource for the first time according to UEBA baselines. First-time access to new resources after credential exposure is a strong indicator of lateral movement by an attacker exploring the environment.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    BehaviorAnalytics
    | where ActivityInsights has "FirstTimeUserAccessedResource"
    | project UserPrincipalName, SourceIPAddress, ResourceAccessed = DestinationDevice, ActivityInsights, TimeGenerated
) on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| project email, SourceIPAddress, ResourceAccessed, ActivityInsights, TimeGenerated
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SourceIPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User UEBA Impossible Travel Detection
properties.description: Leverages UEBA impossible travel analytics for users with known SpyCloud credential exposures. UEBA provides ML-based travel analysis that is more accurate than simple geo-distance calculations.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    BehaviorAnalytics
    | where ActivityInsights has "ImpossibleTravel"
    | project UserPrincipalName, SourceIPAddress, ActivityInsights, InvestigationPriority, SourceIPLocation = SourceLocation, TimeGenerated
) on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| project email, SourceIPAddress, SourceIPLocation, InvestigationPriority, TimeGenerated
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SourceIPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User Anomalous Sign-In Pattern
properties.description: Detects when users with SpyCloud credential exposures exhibit anomalous sign-in patterns identified by UEBA, including unusual times, unusual browsers, unusual device types, or unusual applications accessed.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    BehaviorAnalytics
    | where ActivityInsights has_any ("AnomalousSignIn", "UnusualTime", "UnusualBrowser", "UnusualDevice")
    | project UserPrincipalName, SourceIPAddress, ActivityType, ActivityInsights, TimeGenerated, DevicesInsight
) on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| project email, SourceIPAddress, ActivityType, ActivityInsights, DevicesInsight, TimeGenerated
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SourceIPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User UEBA Investigation Priority Spike
properties.description: Detects when a user with SpyCloud credential exposure has their UEBA investigation priority score increase above 7 (high risk). This combines SpyCloud threat intelligence with Microsoft behavioral analytics for high-confidence threat detection.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    BehaviorAnalytics
    | where InvestigationPriority >= 7
    | summarize MaxPriority = max(InvestigationPriority), Activities = make_set(ActivityType, 10) by UserPrincipalName, SourceIPAddress
) on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| project email, MaxPriority, Activities, SourceIPAddress
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SourceIPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed Credential IP Address in Firewall Deny Logs
properties.description: Correlates IP addresses from SpyCloud infostealer data with firewall deny events in CommonSecurityLog. Indicates the infected device or attacker IP is actively attempting to access your network and being blocked, confirming active threat exploitation.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(ip_addresses)
| mv-expand ip_addresses
| extend SpyCloudIP = tostring(ip_addresses)
| distinct SpyCloudIP, email, infected_machine_id
| join kind=inner (
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where DeviceAction has_any ("Deny", "Drop", "Block", "Reject")
    | project SourceIP, DestinationIP, DeviceVendor, DeviceProduct, Activity, FWTime = TimeGenerated
    | extend MatchIP = SourceIP
) on $left.SpyCloudIP == $right.MatchIP
| project email, SpyCloudIP, infected_machine_id, DeviceVendor, DeviceProduct, Activity, FWTime
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SpyCloudIP

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Infected Device IP Connecting Through Fortinet Firewall
properties.description: Detects when an IP address from a SpyCloud-identified infected device connects through Fortinet FortiGate firewalls. Correlates infostealer infection data with Fortinet FSSO authentication events to identify compromised devices accessing the network.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(ip_addresses)
| mv-expand ip_addresses
| extend SpyCloudIP = tostring(ip_addresses)
| distinct SpyCloudIP, email, infected_machine_id, user_hostname
| join kind=inner (
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where DeviceVendor == "Fortinet"
    | where DeviceAction == "accept" or Activity has "FSSO"
    | project SourceIP, SourceUserName, DeviceProduct, Activity, FWTime = TimeGenerated, DestinationIP, DestinationPort
) on $left.SpyCloudIP == $right.SourceIP
| project email, SpyCloudIP, infected_machine_id, user_hostname, SourceUserName, Activity, FWTime, DestinationIP, DestinationPort
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SpyCloudIP, HostCustomEntity = user_hostname

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Infected Device IP Connecting Through Palo Alto Firewall
properties.description: Detects when an IP address from a SpyCloud-identified infected device connects through Palo Alto Networks firewalls. Correlates infostealer infection data with PAN User-ID events to identify compromised devices with active network access.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(ip_addresses)
| mv-expand ip_addresses
| extend SpyCloudIP = tostring(ip_addresses)
| distinct SpyCloudIP, email, infected_machine_id, user_hostname
| join kind=inner (
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where DeviceVendor == "Palo Alto Networks"
    | where DeviceAction == "allow"
    | project SourceIP, SourceUserName, DeviceProduct, Activity, FWTime = TimeGenerated, DestinationIP, DestinationPort, ApplicationProtocol
) on $left.SpyCloudIP == $right.SourceIP
| project email, SpyCloudIP, infected_machine_id, user_hostname, SourceUserName, Activity, FWTime, DestinationIP, DestinationPort, ApplicationProtocol
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SpyCloudIP, HostCustomEntity = user_hostname

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Infected Device DNS Query to Malware C2 Domain
properties.description: Detects when devices identified as infected by SpyCloud make DNS queries to known malware command-and-control domains. Correlates SpyCloud target_domain (C2 infrastructure) with DNS analytics to identify active C2 communication from compromised endpoints.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(target_domain)
| distinct target_domain, email, infected_machine_id
| join kind=inner (
    DnsEvents
    | where isnotempty(Name)
    | project QueryName = Name, ClientIP, DNSTime = TimeGenerated, QueryType
) on $left.target_domain == $right.QueryName
| project email, target_domain, ClientIP, DNSTime, infected_machine_id, QueryType
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = ClientIP, DomainCustomEntity = target_domain

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User VPN Connection from New Location
properties.description: Detects VPN connections from users with SpyCloud credential exposures originating from geographic locations not seen in their 30-day baseline. Attackers using stolen VPN credentials from unusual locations represent a direct network intrusion vector.
properties.query:
let user_baseline = SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(1d))
| where AppDisplayName has_any ("VPN", "GlobalProtect", "FortiClient", "AnyConnect", "Pulse")
| summarize Locations = make_set(tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion), 50) by UserPrincipalName;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
    | where AppDisplayName has_any ("VPN", "GlobalProtect", "FortiClient", "AnyConnect", "Pulse")
    | where ResultType == 0
    | extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion)
    | project UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Country, VPNTime = TimeGenerated, AppDisplayName
) on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| join kind=leftouter user_baseline on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| where Country !in (Locations)
| project email, IPAddress, Country, VPNTime, AppDisplayName
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = IPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Firewall Allowed Traffic from Exposed IP After Credential Theft
properties.description: Detects allowed firewall connections originating from IPs associated with SpyCloud infostealer infections within 48 hours of exposure. Indicates potential lateral movement where an attacker with stolen credentials is traversing the network perimeter.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(ip_addresses)
| mv-expand ip_addresses
| extend SpyCloudIP = tostring(ip_addresses)
| project ExposureTime = TimeGenerated, SpyCloudIP, email, infected_machine_id
| join kind=inner (
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where DeviceAction in ("allow", "accept", "pass")
    | project SourceIP, DestinationIP, DestinationPort, DeviceVendor, FWTime = TimeGenerated
) on $left.SpyCloudIP == $right.SourceIP
| where FWTime between (ExposureTime .. (ExposureTime + 48h))
| summarize ConnectionCount = count(), DestIPs = make_set(DestinationIP, 10), DestPorts = make_set(DestinationPort, 10) by email, SpyCloudIP, infected_machine_id, DeviceVendor
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SpyCloudIP

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Multi-Stage Attack: Credential Exposure to Risky Sign-In to Data Exfiltration
properties.description: Multi-stage attack detection: Stage 1 - SpyCloud detects credential exposure. Stage 2 - Azure AD detects risky sign-in for the same user. Stage 3 - Office 365 detects data exfiltration (mass download, mail forwarding, eDiscovery). This chain represents a complete account takeover with data theft.
properties.query:
let exposed_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| project email, ExposureTime = TimeGenerated, severity;
let risky_signins = SigninLogs
| where RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ("high", "medium") or RiskLevelAggregated in ("high", "medium")
| where ResultType == 0
| project UserPrincipalName, SignInTime = TimeGenerated, IPAddress, RiskLevel = RiskLevelDuringSignIn;
let exfil_events = OfficeActivity
| where Operation in ("FileDownloaded", "FileSyncDownloadedFull", "New-InboxRule", "Set-InboxRule", "SearchExportDownloaded")
| summarize ExfilCount = count(), Operations = make_set(Operation, 10) by UserId, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h), ClientIP
| where ExfilCount >= 10
| project UserId, ExfilTime = TimeGenerated, ExfilCount, Operations, ClientIP;
exposed_users
| join kind=inner risky_signins on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| where SignInTime > ExposureTime
| join kind=inner exfil_events on $left.email == $right.UserId
| where ExfilTime > SignInTime
| project email, ExposureTime, severity, SignInTime, RiskLevel, IPAddress, ExfilTime, ExfilCount, Operations, ClientIP
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = IPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Multi-Stage Attack: Exposure then MFA Failure then Success
properties.description: Detects a three-stage attack pattern: credential exposure in SpyCloud, followed by failed MFA attempts (attacker testing stolen creds), followed by a successful authentication (MFA bypass achieved via stolen session cookie, social engineering, or MFA fatigue). High confidence account compromise.
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| project email, ExposureTime = TimeGenerated;
let mfa_failures = SigninLogs
| where ResultType == 50074 or ResultType == 50076 or ConditionalAccessStatus == "failure"
| project UserPrincipalName, FailTime = TimeGenerated, IPAddress, FailReason = ResultDescription;
let successful_auth = SigninLogs
| where ResultType == 0
| project UserPrincipalName, SuccessTime = TimeGenerated, SuccessIP = IPAddress, AppDisplayName;
exposed
| join kind=inner mfa_failures on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| where FailTime > ExposureTime
| join kind=inner successful_auth on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| where SuccessTime > FailTime and SuccessTime < (FailTime + 24h)
| summarize FailCount = count(), FailIPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5) by email, ExposureTime, SuccessTime, SuccessIP, AppDisplayName
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SuccessIP

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Multi-Stage Attack: Device Infection to Lateral Movement to Privilege Escalation
properties.description: Detects a three-stage attack: SpyCloud identifies device infection (infostealer), followed by lateral movement indicators (new resource access from the infected device IP), followed by privilege escalation (admin role assignment or elevated permission grant). Represents a full kill chain from initial compromise to domain dominance.
properties.query:
let infected_ips = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(ip_addresses)
| mv-expand ip_addresses
| project InfectedIP = tostring(ip_addresses), email, infected_machine_id, InfectionTime = TimeGenerated;
let lateral_movement = SigninLogs
| where ResultType == 0
| where AppDisplayName has_any ("Azure Portal", "Microsoft Azure Management", "Graph API", "Exchange", "SharePoint")
| project UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, LateralTime = TimeGenerated, AppDisplayName;
let priv_escalation = AuditLogs
| where OperationName has_any ("Add member to role", "Add eligible member to role", "Add app role assignment")
| extend TargetUPN = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
| project TargetUPN, OperationName, PrivTime = TimeGenerated;
infected_ips
| join kind=inner lateral_movement on $left.InfectedIP == $right.IPAddress
| where LateralTime > InfectionTime
| join kind=inner priv_escalation on $left.email == $right.TargetUPN
| where PrivTime > LateralTime
| project email, InfectedIP, infected_machine_id, InfectionTime, LateralTime, AppDisplayName, PrivTime, OperationName
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = InfectedIP

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Remediation Gap: Exposure Without Conditional Access Action (24+ Hours)
properties.description: Detects high-severity SpyCloud exposures (severity >= 20) that have been present for over 24 hours without any corresponding Conditional Access remediation action (password reset, session revocation, CA group assignment). Indicates the automated playbook may have failed or is not configured.
properties.query:
let remediated = SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL
| where ActionStatus == "Success"
| distinct Email;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where TimeGenerated < ago(24h)
| where email !in (remediated)
| summarize
    ExposureAge = datetime_diff('hour', now(), min(TimeGenerated)),
    ExposureCount = count(),
    MaxSeverity = max(severity),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10)
  by email, infected_machine_id
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- User Re-Exposed After Password Reset
properties.description: Detects when a user who previously had their password reset through the CA playbook appears in new SpyCloud exposures. Indicates the user may have set a weak password again, the device is still infected and stealing new credentials, or a different device is compromised.
properties.query:
SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL
| where PasswordReset == true and ActionStatus == "Success"
| project ResetEmail = Email, ResetTime = TimeGenerated
| join kind=inner (
    SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
    | where severity >= 20
    | project email, NewExposureTime = TimeGenerated, severity, infected_machine_id, target_domain
) on $left.ResetEmail == $right.email
| where NewExposureTime > ResetTime
| summarize
    NewExposures = count(),
    DaysSinceReset = datetime_diff('day', min(NewExposureTime), max(ResetTime)),
    NewDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10)
  by email, infected_machine_id
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Conditional Access Policy Bypass Attempt by Exposed User
properties.description: Detects when a user with SpyCloud credential exposure attempts to sign in and triggers a Conditional Access policy failure, followed by a successful sign-in. This pattern may indicate an attacker switching to a compliant device or using a legacy authentication protocol to bypass CA controls.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| distinct email
| join kind=inner (
    SigninLogs
    | where ConditionalAccessStatus == "failure"
    | project UserPrincipalName, FailTime = TimeGenerated, FailIP = IPAddress, CAPolicy = tostring(ConditionalAccessPolicies)
) on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| join kind=inner (
    SigninLogs
    | where ResultType == 0
    | project UserPrincipalName, SuccessTime = TimeGenerated, SuccessIP = IPAddress, AppDisplayName, ClientAppUsed
) on $left.email == $right.UserPrincipalName
| where SuccessTime between (FailTime .. (FailTime + 4h))
| project email, FailTime, FailIP, SuccessTime, SuccessIP, AppDisplayName, ClientAppUsed
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SuccessIP

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Exposed User Successful Access Despite Conditional Access Block
properties.description: Detects when a user known to be in the SpyCloud CA remediation group (should be blocked by Conditional Access) successfully authenticates. Indicates a CA policy misconfiguration, exclusion, or legacy auth bypass that is allowing compromised users to access resources.
properties.query:
SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL
| where AddedToCAGroup == true and ActionStatus == "Success"
| project BlockedEmail = Email, GroupAddTime = TimeGenerated
| join kind=inner (
    SigninLogs
    | where ResultType == 0
    | project UserPrincipalName, SignInTime = TimeGenerated, IPAddress, AppDisplayName, ClientAppUsed, ConditionalAccessStatus
) on $left.BlockedEmail == $right.UserPrincipalName
| where SignInTime > GroupAddTime
| project email = BlockedEmail, GroupAddTime, SignInTime, IPAddress, AppDisplayName, ClientAppUsed, ConditionalAccessStatus
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = IPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Infected IP Address Found in DNS Query Logs
properties.description: Correlates IP addresses from SpyCloud infostealer data with DNS query logs to identify infected devices actively resolving domains. Helps identify compromised endpoints that may still be communicating with C2 infrastructure or exfiltrating data via DNS.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(ip_addresses)
| mv-expand ip_addresses
| extend SpyCloudIP = tostring(ip_addresses)
| distinct SpyCloudIP, email, infected_machine_id
| join kind=inner (
    DnsEvents
    | project ClientIP, QueryName = Name, DNSTime = TimeGenerated, QueryType
    | where isnotempty(ClientIP)
) on $left.SpyCloudIP == $right.ClientIP
| summarize
    QueryCount = count(),
    UniqueDomains = dcount(QueryName),
    TopDomains = make_set(QueryName, 20)
  by email, SpyCloudIP, infected_machine_id
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SpyCloudIP

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Infected Host Communicating with Threat Intelligence Matched Domain
properties.description: Detects when SpyCloud-identified infected devices communicate with domains flagged in Microsoft Sentinel Threat Intelligence feeds. Dual-source correlation (SpyCloud + TI) provides very high confidence that the device is actively compromised and communicating with known malicious infrastructure.
properties.query:
let ti_domains = ThreatIntelligenceIndicator
| where isnotempty(DomainName)
| where ExpirationDateTime > now()
| where Active == true
| distinct DomainName;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(target_domain)
| where target_domain in (ti_domains)
| summarize
    ExposureCount = count(),
    Emails = make_set(email, 10),
    Devices = make_set(infected_machine_id, 5)
  by target_domain
| mv-expand Emails
| extend AccountCustomEntity = tostring(Emails), DomainCustomEntity = target_domain

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Network Anomaly from Compromised Endpoint
properties.description: Detects anomalous network traffic patterns from endpoints identified as compromised by SpyCloud. Correlates with Azure Firewall logs to identify unusual outbound connections, high data volumes, or connections to suspicious destinations from infected devices.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(ip_addresses)
| mv-expand ip_addresses
| extend SpyCloudIP = tostring(ip_addresses)
| distinct SpyCloudIP, email, infected_machine_id
| join kind=inner (
    AzureDiagnostics
    | where Category == "AzureFirewallNetworkRule" or Category == "AzureFirewallApplicationRule"
    | where msg_s has "Allow" or msg_s has "Deny"
    | extend SourceIP = extract("from ([0-9.]+):", 1, msg_s)
    | extend DestIP = extract("to ([0-9.]+):", 1, msg_s)
    | extend DestPort = extract(":([0-9]+)", 1, extract("to [0-9.]+:([0-9]+)", 0, msg_s))
    | project SourceIP, DestIP, DestPort, msg_s, FWTime = TimeGenerated
) on $left.SpyCloudIP == $right.SourceIP
| summarize
    ConnectionCount = count(),
    UniqueDestinations = dcount(DestIP),
    DestPorts = make_set(DestPort, 20)
  by email, SpyCloudIP, infected_machine_id
| where ConnectionCount > 50 or UniqueDestinations > 20
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, IPCustomEntity = SpyCloudIP

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Infected Machine Active in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud infected machine identifiers and hostnames with active devices in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint DeviceInfo table. Confirms that SpyCloud-identified infected devices are still active on the corporate network and provides MDE device context for investigation.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(user_hostname)
| summarize
    ExposureCount = count(),
    MaxSeverity = max(severity),
    Emails = make_set(email, 10),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10)
  by user_hostname, infected_machine_id, user_os
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceInfo
    | where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
    | summarize LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated) by DeviceName, OSPlatform, PublicIP, DeviceId
) on $left.user_hostname == $right.DeviceName
| project user_hostname, infected_machine_id, ExposureCount, MaxSeverity, Emails, user_os, OSPlatform, PublicIP, DeviceId, LastSeen
| extend HostCustomEntity = user_hostname, IPCustomEntity = PublicIP

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Credential Theft Tool Execution on Exposed Device
properties.description: Detects when credential theft tools (mimikatz, LaZagne, credential dumpers) execute on devices identified as infected by SpyCloud. Confirms active attacker post-exploitation activity on a known compromised endpoint, indicating the threat has progressed beyond initial credential theft.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(user_hostname)
| distinct user_hostname, email, infected_machine_id
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where FileName has_any ("mimikatz", "lazagne", "procdump", "nanodump", "pypykatz", "secretsdump", "lsass", "comsvcs")
        or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("sekurlsa", "logonpasswords", "lsass.dmp", "credential", "vault::cred", "dpapi::masterkey")
    | project DeviceName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, ProcessTime = TimeGenerated, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName
) on $left.user_hostname == $right.DeviceName
| project email, user_hostname, infected_machine_id, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, ProcessTime, AccountName
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, HostCustomEntity = user_hostname

properties.displayName: SpyCloud -- Lateral Movement Detected from Compromised Device
properties.description: Detects lateral movement activity (remote logons, SMB connections, WMI, PSExec, RDP) originating from devices identified as infected by SpyCloud. Indicates an attacker is using stolen credentials from the infected device to access other systems in the network.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(user_hostname)
| distinct user_hostname, email, infected_machine_id
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceLogonEvents
    | where LogonType in ("RemoteInteractive", "Network", "NewCredentials")
    | where IsLocalAdmin == false or ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
    | project DeviceName, RemoteDeviceName, AccountName, LogonType, LogonTime = TimeGenerated, RemoteIP
    | where isnotempty(RemoteDeviceName) and DeviceName != RemoteDeviceName
) on $left.user_hostname == $right.DeviceName
| summarize
    LateralMoveCount = count(),
    TargetDevices = make_set(RemoteDeviceName, 10),
    LogonTypes = make_set(LogonType, 5)
  by email, user_hostname, infected_machine_id, AccountName
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, HostCustomEntity = user_hostname
